**Crawler SSRF:**
- Scrapy Request() constructed from untrusted input (llms.txt contents, user input)
- allowed_domains not set or overly broad
- start_urls pointing to internal IPs (127.0.0.1, 169.254.x, 10.x, 172.16-31.x)
- Redirects to internal resources not blocked
- DNS rebinding attacks via crafted hostnames

**Crawler Data Leakage:**
- Crawled content written to paths outside .gitignore coverage
- API keys or tokens appearing in crawled HTML stored in cache/output
- DATABASE_URL or connection strings in log output
- Raw response bodies containing secrets persisted to Neon without sanitization
- Scrapy LOG_FORMAT exposing auth headers or cookies

**Cache Poisoning:**
- pickle.loads() on data from Neon http_cache table (deserialization attack surface)
- Cached responses served without validating stored content-type
- Cache key collisions allowing response substitution
- Stale cache served after TTL expiration due to clock skew

**SQL Injection in Neon Pipelines:**
- String interpolation in SQL queries (f-strings, .format(), % operator with dicts)
- Unsanitized spider names or URLs in INSERT statements
- Dynamic table names constructed from user input

**Dependency Confusion:**
- npm packages installed from unexpected registries
- pip packages with typosquatting names
- Vendored repos containing their own .npmrc or .pypirc with alternate registries

**Credential Exposure in Vendored Code:**
- .git directories remaining after vendor strip
- GitHub tokens in .github/workflows of vendored repos
- API keys in test fixtures or example files of vendored code
- OAuth tokens in config files (credentials.json, auth.json)
